findings.evidence: WeilDivisor.lean:489 `(h : w x = w y) : pointDifference x y ∈ weightedDegreeZeroSubgroup w`; BasepointChange.lean:77 proves the new lemma by `simpa using pointDifference_mem_weightedDegreeZeroSubgroup ... h`.
findings.fix: Delete the wrapper and call the existing (already @[simp]) pointDifference_mem_weightedDegreeZeroSubgroup directly at the one use site (line 91).
findings.issue: basepointDifference_mem_weightedDegreeZeroSubgroup is an exact restatement of WeilDivisor.lean:489 pointDifference_mem_weightedDegreeZeroSubgroup (identical hypothesis w a = w b, identical conclusion); both are @[simp], so the new lemma adds only a redundant name and a duplicate simp rule.

findings.evidence: Diff adds `@[expose]` to the def; BasepointChange.lean weightedPointBaseDifference_change_base proves the identity via `simp [weightedPointBaseDifference, …]`, and coeff_weightedPointBaseDifference (WeilDivisor.lean:513) already characterizes it.
findings.fix: Drop @[expose] and instead add a `weightedPointBaseDifference_def : weightedPointBaseDifference w x₀ x = ofPoint x - w x • ofPoint x₀ := rfl` in WeilDivisor.lean (or rely on the existing @[simp] coeff_weightedPointBaseDifference), then rewrite the downstream proofs through it.
findings.issue: @[expose] is newly added to weightedPointBaseDifference solely so the downstream file can unfold it (simp [weightedPointBaseDifference, …]) when proving the change-base identities; this exposes the implementation body to compensate for a missing characteristic lemma.

summary: Characteristic API for the two new base-point-change classes is complete and well-annotated, but the PR adds a verbatim-duplicate degree-zero membership lemma and exposes an implementation body where a characteristic lemma suffices.
